bio.short: Final-year Biochemistry student at IPB University with hands-on experience in laboratory testing, analytical evaluation, and data documentation.
bio.long: Final-year Biochemistry student at IPB University with hands-on experience in laboratory testing, analytical evaluation, and data documentation. Skilled in operating analytical instruments, maintaining accurate records, and working in accordance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP). Detail-oriented, highly organized, and eager to contribute to quality assessment and sensory evaluation. Proficient in Microsoft Excel and enthusiastic about learning in laboratory and production-line environments.

education.description: Final-year student focusing on biomolecular analysis, plant biochemistry, and Good Laboratory Practice.

projects.title: Independent Pilot Program
projects.subtitle: Horticultural Therapy Research
projects.description: Designed and implemented a horticultural therapy program at Rumah Azaki — a social welfare institution in Bogor — to develop self-care skills and social competencies for individuals with intellectual and mental disabilities. Demonstrated up to 80% improvement in planting activities for intellectually disabled participants.

experiences.description: Guided and supervised 12 students during laboratory practical sessions on carbohydrate, protein, lipid, and nucleic acid analysis, including starch and lipid hydrolysis, protein and nucleic acid denaturation, and biomolecule characterization. Operated and assisted in the use of laboratory instruments, including a UV–Vis spectrophotometer, PCR machine, and agarose gel electrophoresis system. Conducted student assessments in accordance with the course contract.

experiences.role: Nucleic Acid and Protein Engineering Laboratory Assistant
experiences.description: Guided and supervised 11 students during gene isolation and cloning sessions of the amylase-coding gene from Bacillus licheniformis, covering genomic DNA extraction, PCR-based cloning, agarose gel electrophoresis, and plasmid DNA digestion. Operated laboratory instruments including a PCR machine, NanoDrop spectrophotometer, and agarose gel electrophoresis system. Conducted student assessments in accordance with the course contract.

Assisted analysis of 30+ salak leaf DNA samples using gradient PCR and agarose gel electrophoresis. Assisted analysis of 41 oil palm leaf DNA samples using PCR and polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (PAGE). Supported oil palm self-DNA tissue culture research by contributing to the preparation of 100+ oil palm seedling samples, including sterilization and observation.

Performed moisture and phosphate analyses on 30+ plant samples using gravimetric and UV-VIS spectrophotometry, and prepared essential laboratory equipment, buffers, and chemical reagents. Conducted protein and xylanase activity assays on 30+ service samples, ensuring accurate data generation. Maintained compliant raw material storage and laboratory waste handling in accordance with safety and environmental protocols.

Conducted proximate and phytochemical analyses while strictly implementing Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) standards to ensure data accuracy and laboratory safety, including moisture/ash determination and antioxidant, flavonoid, and phenolic quantification using UV–VIS spectrophotometry. Performed identification and characterization of 10+ samples using FTIR and TLC spectroscopy for chemical fingerprinting.

experiences.company: Student Executive Board FMIPA - IPB University
experiences.description: Planned the main work program concepts, G-astip and Tutor BEM FMIPA, by organizing profit-sharing arrangements and implementation systems for both programs. Achieved over 20 G-astip customers and successfully engaged 5 students in the Tutor BEM FMIPA. Achieved a profit of Rp8.000.000,- in 10 months of management from an initial capital of Rp400.000,-.

experiences.role: Secretary of the Administrative Affairs Bureau
experiences.company: IPB Sustainable Science Research Student Association
experiences.description: Documented meeting minutes and prepared evaluation reports for the Bureau of Administrative Affairs. Monitored and reviewed the budget plan (RKAT) and meeting records under the Directorate of Talent, Partnership, and External Communication.

publications.title: Pengembangan Bina Diri dan Keterampilan Sosial Penyandang Disabilitas Intelektual dan Mental Melalui Program Independent Pilot
publications.venue: AGROKREATIF: Jurnal Ilmiah Pengabdian kepada Masyarakat
publications.abstract: Rumah Azaki, a social welfare institution in Bogor Regency, faces challenges in facilitating individuals with disabilities to develop self-care skills and social competencies. The Independent Pilot program was designed to address these issues through a horticultural therapy approach, demonstrating up to 80% improvement in planting activities for intellectually disabled participants.

awards.title: Scientific Writing Competition 13th Airlangga Ideas Competition 2025
Business of The Year - Student Entrepreneurship Program (Batiknese)
National Student Creativity Program in Social Humanities Research (PKM-RSH) 2023
